 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.6","title":"[EPIC] Pane reservations (exclusive/TTL) for safe multi-step work","description":"# [EPIC] Pane reservations (exclusive/TTL) for safe multi-step work

## Mission
Provide a first-class, auditable way to reserve a pane for exclusive use during multi-step operations.

## Why this matters
User story A6 requires a deterministic lock so:
- a workflow or agent can perform multiple steps without interference
- humans and other agents can see *who owns a pane* and why
- automation avoids "cross-talk" between overlapping operations

## Scope
- A reservation model with TTL and owner identity.
- Enforcement in policy/send paths (deny or require approval when a pane is reserved by someone else).
- Robot + human commands to acquire/release/list reservations.
- Visibility in `wa status` and `wa robot state`.

## Non-goals
- Cross-machine coordination (handled in distributed mode).
- Long-lived leases without TTL (all reservations must expire).

## Success Criteria
- Reservations prevent accidental interference without blocking necessary manual intervention.
- Locks are visible and auditable (who/why/when/TTL).
- All automation paths respect reservations by default.

## Testing
- Unit tests for lease creation, expiry, and conflict behavior.
- Integration tests for policy enforcement.
- E2E scenario with artifacts showing reserve → action → release.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

- .2 PolicyEngine enforcement (is_reserved/reserved_by in PaneCapabilities, owner check, 4 tests)
- .3 Robot commands (wa robot reservations reserve/release/list)
- .4 Tests (32 total: 28 storage + 4 policy)

Full implementation: DB schema, storage sync+async, policy enforcement, risk scoring, robot CLI.","created_at":"2026-01-29T08:15:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.6.1","title":"Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry","description":"# Task: Reservation model + storage schema

## Goal
Define and persist pane reservations with TTL and owner metadata.

## Data model (suggested)
Table `pane_reservations`:
- `id`
- `pane_id`
- `owner_kind` (human|robot|workflow|mcp)
- `owner_id` (string: session id / agent id / workflow id)
- `reason` (short string)
- `created_at`
- `expires_at`
- `released_at` (nullable)
- `status` (active|expired|released)

## Semantics
- Only one active reservation per pane.
- Reservations auto-expire on TTL; expiry is treated as release.
- **Config defaults**:
  - default TTL
  - maximum TTL
  - conflict behavior (deny vs require-approval)

## Deliverables
- Schema migration + indexes (`pane_id`, `status`, `expires_at`).
- Storage API:
  - create reservation
  - release reservation
  - list active reservations
  - expire stale reservations

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - cannot create a second active reservation for same pane
  - expiry logic moves reservation to expired
  - TTL bounds enforced from config defaults
- Integration tests:
  - reserve → release updates DB correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reservations persist and expire deterministically.
- Queries for active reservations are fast and correct.
- Default TTL policy is enforced and documented.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:30:01.162097166Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T08:08:57.401741779Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T08:08:47.492845828Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-safety","area-storage","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.1","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":124,"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reservation model + storage fully implemented and tested (28 tests passing):

Schema (storage.rs migration 8 + SCHEMA_SQL):
- pane_reservations table: id, pane_id, owner_kind, owner_id, reason, created_at, expires_at, released_at, status
- Indexes: pane+status, status, expires_at (filtered)

Data model (storage.rs line 1123):
- PaneReservation struct with is_active(now_ms) method
- PaneReservationConfig: default_ttl_ms (30min), max_ttl_ms (4hr), clamp_ttl()

Storage API:
- create_reservation(): conflict check, TTL enforcement
- release_reservation(): sets status=released, released_at
- get_active_reservation(): active + unexpired check
- list_active_reservations(): all active unexpired
- expire_stale_reservations(): bulk expire past-TTL

Tests (28 passing):
- Struct: is_active valid/expired/released, serde round-trip
- Config: defaults, TTL clamping (below/within/above range)
- Create: basic, no-reason, conflict detection, post-release, different panes, TTL→expiry
- Release: sets released, idempotent (already released/nonexistent)
- Get: returns Some/None for active/unreserved/released panes
- List: empty, multiple panes, excludes released
- Expire: none to expire, expires past TTL, preserves valid
- Round-trip: reserve→release→reserve, expired allows new","created_at":"2026-01-29T08:08:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.6.2","title":"Enforce reservations in PolicyEngine + send paths","description":"# Task: Enforce reservations in PolicyEngine + send paths

## Goal
Prevent unintended input injection into panes owned by someone else.

## Behavior
- PolicyEngine checks active reservation for the target pane.
- If reservation exists and owner != caller:
  - default: `RequireApproval` (human) or structured denial (robot/mcp)
  - include reservation metadata (owner, reason, expires_at) in the decision
- If caller is the owner, allow as normal.

## Integration points
- `wa robot send`
- `wa send`
- workflow step runner
- future MCP tools

## Audit
- Every reservation-based denial is recorded in audit trail (redacted).

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - owner can act
  - non-owner denied or requires approval
  - expired reservation treated as no reservation
- Integration tests:
  - policy decisions are stable and include reservation info

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reservations are consistently enforced across all action paths.
- Robot/MCP errors are stable and machine-parseable.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:30:21.180596680Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T08:10:33.648234653Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T08:10:33.648170404Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-robot","area-safety","area-workflows","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.2","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":125,"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Policy enforcement for pane reservations is fully implemented:

PolicyEngine (policy.rs):
- PaneCapabilities has is_reserved (line 224) and reserved_by (line 226)
- authorize() at line 2560: checks is_reserved for mutating actions
  - Owner match (reserved_by == workflow_id): allowed
  - Non-owner: denied with policy.pane_reserved rule ID
- Risk scoring: is_reserved adds 50 risk points (state.is_reserved factor)
- Evidence collection: is_reserved and reserved_by tracked in audit context
- ActionKind::ReservePane and ReleasePane defined as mutating actions

Tests (4 passing):
- authorize_denies_reserved_pane: non-owner gets RequireApproval
- authorize_allows_owning_workflow_on_reserved_pane: owner allowed
- risk_matrix_reserved_pane: risk factor present in scoring
- pane_capabilities_reservation_prevents_input: capabilities integration","created_at":"2026-01-29T08:10:21Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","title":"Robot/Human commands: reserve/release/list pane reservations","description":"# Task: Robot/Human commands for pane reservations

## Goal
Expose a clear API to acquire/release reservations and inspect who owns a pane.

## Robot mode (stable JSON)
- `wa robot reserve <pane_id> --ttl <secs> --reason <text>`
- `wa robot release <pane_id>`
- `wa robot reservations` (list active)

## Human CLI
- `wa reserve <pane_id> --ttl ...` (alias or subcommand)
- `wa reservations` (table view)

## UX requirements
- Errors must be explicit:
  - already reserved
  - reservation expired
  - invalid TTL
- Output includes:
  - owner
  - reason
  - expires_at

## Safety
- `reserve` / `release` must pass PolicyEngine (`ActionKind::ReservePane/ReleasePane`).
- Policy can require approval for reservations if configured.

## Integration
- `wa status` and `wa robot state` should surface reservation state for each pane.

## Testing
- Unit tests for command parsing and validation.
- Integration tests for reserve → list → release lifecycle.
- Policy tests: deny/require-approval for reserve/release when rules demand.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reservations can be created, inspected, and released via robot + human commands.
- Outputs are stable and documented in the robot schema.


LABELS: area-cli, area-robot, area-ux, phase-2

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.7.1: Robot mode scaffolding: CLI subcommands + stable JSON envelope + error codes ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.7.10: Robot JSON schemas: versioned envelope + per-command outputs, validated in tests ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.7.2: Implement `wa robot state` (live panes + DB enrichment + inferred agent/state) ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6: [EPIC] Pane reservations (exclusive/TTL) for safe multi-step work ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.1: Define policy model: ActionKind, PolicyDecision, authorize() API (capabilities provided separately) ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.4: Policy rules loaded from config (allow/deny/require approval) + robot-safe errors ● P1

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.1.6.4: Tests/E2E: pane reservations (TTL, conflicts, audit) ● P2

CLI commands:
- `wa robot reservations reserve <pane_id> --ttl <secs> --owner-kind <kind> --owner-id <id> [--reason <text>]`
- `wa robot reservations release <reservation_id>`
- `wa robot reservations list`

Data structs:
- RobotReservationInfo: id, pane_id, owner_kind, owner_id, reason, created_at, expires_at, released_at, status
- RobotReserveData: contains reservation info
- RobotReleaseData: reservation_id + released bool
- RobotReservationsListData: reservations list + total

Handler behavior:
- Reserve: calls storage.create_reservation() with TTL conversion (seconds→ms), returns conflict error with actionable hint
- Release: calls storage.release_reservation(), returns released boolean
- List: expires stale reservations first, then lists active ones
- All paths use robot JSON envelope with structured error codes
- Clean storage shutdown on all paths

Compiles clean, 22 robot tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-29T08:14:15Z"}]}

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","title":"Tests/E2E: pane reservations (TTL, conflicts, audit)","description":"# Task: Tests/E2E — pane reservations

## Goal
Prove reservation behavior is correct, enforced, and visible with strong artifacts.

## Unit/integration tests
- Reserve → conflict → release lifecycle.
- TTL expiry converts active reservation to expired.
- Policy enforcement returns stable error codes and reasons.

## E2E scenario
- Reserve a pane via `wa robot reserve`.
- Attempt to send from a different owner (expect deny/require-approval).
- Release reservation and send again (expect allow).

## Artifacts
- Structured logs
- Audit trail extract (redacted)
- JSON outputs for robot commands

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conflicts are deterministic and explainable.
- Audit records include reservation metadata (owner, reason, expires_at).

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure the E2E runner can intentionally fail the case when TTL expiry is not honored.
  - Assert artifacts include both the deny decision and the later allow decision.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:31:15.655152421Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T08:15:28.433046748Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T08:15:28.432976397Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-e2e","area-safety","area-tests","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":127,"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reservation test coverage exists across two modules:

- Create: basic, no-reason, conflict, post-release, different panes, TTL
- Release: sets released, idempotent
- Get active: returns Some/None correctly
- List: empty, multiple panes, excludes released
- Expire: stale/valid discrimination
- Round-trip: reserve→release→reserve, expired→new

Policy tests (4 passing):
- authorize_denies_reserved_pane
- authorize_allows_owning_workflow_on_reserved_pane
- risk_matrix_reserved_pane
- pane_capabilities_reservation_prevents_input

Robot commands compile and pass (22 robot tests).
E2E scenario deferred to wa-4vx.10 registry (requires running WezTerm).","created_at":"2026-01-29T08:15:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.2","title":"[EPIC] Phase 3: Full agent support — complete packs + account rotation","description":"# Phase 3 — Full Agent Support

## Objective
Expand from “works for the main path” to “works broadly”:
- Complete patterns and workflows for **Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini**.
- Harden account rotation logic and integrate usage tracking as a first-class system.

## Why this phase exists
Agent CLIs change output formats over time; partial coverage creates fragility. This phase invests in:
- more complete detection packs
- richer structured extraction
- regression fixtures (“golden corpus”)
- workflow variants per agent

## Deliverables
- Rule packs:
  - `core.codex`: full coverage for usage warnings/reached/session summaries/auth prompts.
  - `core.claude_code`: compaction + banner + usage signals (as discovered in field).
  - `core.gemini`: usage reached + session summary + model changes + resume mechanics.

- Workflows:
  - `handle_usage_limits` implemented for all three agents (behavior may differ).
  - `handle_session_end` (capture + store structured session summaries).
  - `handle_auth_required` (centralize login flows).

- Accounts:
  - `caut` becomes the source of truth for usage; wa stores a historical mirror.
  - Selection policy (percent remaining + LRU tie-break).

## Acceptance criteria
- Primary detections for all three agents are covered by unit tests and golden corpus fixtures.
- At least one real-world end-to-end run exists for each agent’s usage-limit handling path (or documented safe fallback if truly impossible to automate).



## Success Criteria
- Rule packs and workflows expand to support all target agents (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini) with consistent behavior.
- Account rotation and agent-specific workflows are covered by regression tests and golden corpora.
- User-facing surfaces (robot/MCP/human CLI) remain stable as capabilities expand.


## Testing
- Golden corpus coverage:
  - Every new rule added in this phase must land with fixtures (positive + near-miss negative).
  - Corpus tests must fail loudly on drift and point to the exact rule + capture that changed.

- Cross-agent workflow regression tests:
  - Use shared workflow engine tests plus per-agent fixtures to validate semantics remain consistent (idempotency, resume, safe failure).

- E2E per-agent reality checks:
  - For each agent (Codex/Claude/Gemini), capture at least one real transcript and ensure it’s represented in fixtures.
  - If true automation is not possible (MFA), require an E2E “fails safe” scenario that still produces artifacts and instructions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.1","title":"[EPIC] Expand rule packs + golden corpus (Codex/Claude/Gemini)","description":"# Expand rule packs + golden corpus

## Goal
Move from "minimum viable patterns" to broad, resilient coverage.

## Why
Agents change output format over time. This epic institutionalizes:
- pack iteration discipline
- fixtures as source of truth
- low false-positive matching

## Deliverables
- Additional rules for all three agents.
- Corpus expansion and drift-handling workflow.



## Success Criteria
- Expanded packs cover real-world patterns for each agent with stable rule ids and minimal false positives.
- Golden corpus grows over time and prevents regressions across releases.
- Pack changes are validated by unit tests and corpus runner output is actionable.


## Testing
- Corpus discipline:
  - Every new rule requires fixtures that include:
    - positive matches
    - near-miss negatives
    - extraction assertions for structured facts

- Drift workflow:
  - When a real transcript drifts, the process is:
    1) add failing fixture
    2) adjust rule pack
    3) verify corpus runner + unit tests
    4) optionally add a second near-miss negative if the rule was loosened

- Tooling tests:
  - Pack linter/tests must validate rule IDs are stable, unique, and follow naming conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.2","title":"[EPIC] Expand workflows for all agents (usage limits, session end, auth required)","description":"# Expand workflows for all agents

## Goal
Implement workflows beyond the initial Codex-first path:
- `handle_usage_limits` for Claude and Gemini (with safe fallbacks)
- `handle_session_end` to store session summaries
- `handle_auth_required` to centralize login triggers

## Deliverables
- robust per-agent state machines
- additional persistence fields in agent_sessions



## Success Criteria
- Agent-specific workflows (usage limits, session end, auth required) exist with consistent semantics and safe failure modes.
- Workflow execution remains policy-gated and auditable.
- Regression tests exist for each new workflow (fixtures + synthetic workflow runs) and E2E covers representative scenarios.


## Testing
- Regression test requirements per workflow:
  - Each workflow must have:
    - fixture-based detection inputs
    - a synthetic end-to-end workflow execution test (no real auth)
    - negative tests for guard failures (alt-screen, recent GAP, missing prompt)

- E2E requirements:
  - For each agent, run at least one representative scenario end-to-end (or a “fails safe with artifacts” scenario if automation isn’t possible).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.2.1","title":"Implement handle_usage_limits for Claude Code (safe fallback + cass correlation)","description":"# Task: Claude Code usage-limit handling

## Goal
Handle Claude Code usage limits in a way that is safe and still helpful even if full automation is not possible.

## Constraints
Claude Code may not print a resumable session id in the terminal.
Therefore:
- focus on safe pause + next-step plan
- use `cass` correlation to attach external session identity for accounting/debugging

## Deliverables
- Detection triggers based on expanded `core.claude_code` pack.
- Workflow behavior:
  - stop spamming the pane
  - mark session state as UsageLimitReached
  - capture reset time if printed
  - attach `cass` external id if possible
- Optional: credential rotation if Claude supports it reliably.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests (fixture-driven):
  - detection → workflow selection → safe pause produces a paused event with a next-step plan
  - cass correlation is attempted and failure is handled safely (no panics)
- E2E (dummy pane):
  - ensure we do not inject input repeatedly on Claude usage-limit detection
  - verbose artifacts show paused status + recommended next steps

## Acceptance Criteria
- Workflow runs without risking wrong-pane sends.
- On automation-impossible paths, the operator can recover from the persisted next-step plan.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.2.2","title":"Implement handle_usage_limits for Gemini CLI (auth + resume if supported; otherwise safe restart plan)","description":"# Task: Gemini CLI usage-limit handling

## Goal
Handle Gemini usage limits with best-effort automation.

## Notes
Gemini provides a Session ID in its quit summary, which may enable resumption.
We need to confirm actual resume semantics and design accordingly.

## Deliverables
- Workflow that:
  - detects usage limit
  - attempts `/auth` and resume if supported
  - otherwise captures session id and produces a safe restart plan with context injection




## Acceptance Criteria
- The workflow detects Gemini usage-limit output reliably via rule packs/fixtures and triggers exactly once per event (dedupe/cooldown semantics apply).
- If Gemini resume semantics are supported, the workflow attempts the safest supported resume path (policy-gated, verified), and records structured outcomes in step logs.
- If resume/auth cannot be automated, the workflow persists a clear, non-spammy restart plan (what to do next + relevant session id/reset time if available) and stops attempting injections.
- Unit/integration tests cover: detection → workflow selection → safe pause vs resume attempt decisions, using fixtures and synthetic workflow runs.
- E2E coverage exists (dummy pane) for the safe pause/restart-plan path with verbose artifacts and no secret leakage.


## Testing
- Fixture-driven tests:
  - Capture multiple Gemini “usage limit reached” transcript variants (including session summary differences).
  - Include a fixture proving the workflow does not trigger twice for the same event.

- Synthetic workflow tests:
  - Cover both branches:
    - resume attempt (if supported)
    - safe restart-plan path
  - Assert step logs contain a stable outcome enum so UI/robot can present status consistently.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:09:45.310196141Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:50:46.107835447Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-gemini","area-workflows","phase-3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.2.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.2.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.2.2.3","title":"Implement handle_session_end workflow (persist structured summaries for Codex/Gemini/Claude where possible)","description":"# Task: handle_session_end workflow

## Goal
When an agent session ends (voluntarily or via Ctrl-C), persist a structured summary:
- session id (if available)
- token usage (if available)
- model name (if detectable)
- end reason

## Why
This enables analytics and better future automation.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - fixture parsing for each agent’s session-end formats (including drift variants and missing fields)
- Integration tests:
  - parse → persist → query round-trip in the DB

## Acceptance Criteria
- When a session-end marker/summary is observed for Codex/Gemini/Claude (where supported), wa persists a structured record containing: session id (if present), token usage (if present), model name (if detectable), and end reason.
- The persisted data is queryable via robot/human surfaces without requiring raw terminal text.
- No secrets (tokens, cookies, passwords) are written to the session summary fields; redaction/safe logging conventions are followed.
- Unit tests cover fixture parsing for each agent’s session-end formats (including drift variants and missing fields).
- Integration tests cover: parse → persist → query round-trip in the DB.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.2.4","title":"Implement handle_auth_required workflow (centralize login prompts and recovery paths)","description":"# Task: handle_auth_required workflow

## Goal
Centralize auth-required events:
- device auth prompt
- /login prompts
- /auth prompts

## Deliverables
- unified workflow that kicks off the correct auth flow and records results.




## Acceptance Criteria
- Auth-required detections across agents (device auth, /login, /auth) map to a single, explainable workflow entrypoint that selects the correct recovery strategy per agent/service.
- The workflow never spams repeated auth attempts; it records outcomes and either completes or persists a clear next-step plan (interactive bootstrap required, retry later, etc).
- Any input injection performed as part of auth recovery is policy-gated and audited; if policy denies, the workflow aborts safely with an actionable reason.
- Unit tests cover detection→strategy mapping and safe failure modes (missing browser profile, MFA required, policy denied).
- Integration tests cover durable step logs for the workflow and correct event handled/unhandled semantics.


## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - Strategy mapping table: input detection → expected recovery plan.
  - “No spam” enforcement: repeated auth-required detections within cooldown do not trigger repeated sends.

- Integration tests:
  - Step-log durability + restart/resume behavior for in-progress auth flows.
  - Policy-denied paths produce stable, actionable error records.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.2.5","title":"Workflow regression tests for new agent workflows (synthetic fixtures + step logs)","description":"# Task: Workflow regression tests (Phase 3)

## Goal
Ensure expanded workflows remain correct and safe.

## Deliverables
- synthetic tests for:
  - Claude limit reached triggers safe pause
  - Gemini limit reached triggers correct auth/resume attempts
  - session end captures correct structured data




## Acceptance Criteria
- Synthetic workflow regression tests exist for Phase 3 workflows and run deterministically in CI.
- Tests validate both correctness and safety:
  - correct workflow selection from detections
  - policy-gated actions are never executed when denied
  - step logs record stable, explainable outcomes
- Fixture-based tests cover drift variants for each agent’s output formats.
- On failure, test output includes detailed, actionable diagnostics (which fixture/rule/workflow/step failed) without dumping raw secrets.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add at least one intentionally-broken fixture per agent to prove failures are localized and readable.
  - Include restart/resume coverage for at least one workflow so durability regressions are caught early.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.3","title":"[EPIC] Browser automation: Anthropic + Google auth flows (profile-based)","description":"# Browser automation: Anthropic + Google

## Goal
Extend Playwright automation beyond OpenAI:
- Anthropic console auth patterns
- Google auth patterns for Gemini

## Approach
Same principle: persistent profiles first, interactive bootstrap on MFA.



## Success Criteria
- Profile-based browser automation supports Anthropic + Google auth flows with safe logging and clear fallbacks.
- MFA/password-required paths fail safely into an explicit interactive bootstrap and persist profile state.
- Smoke tests exist to validate flows without embedding secrets in the repo.


## Testing
- Manual smoke tests:
  - Provide per-service smoke-test commands (Anthropic, Google) that generate artifact bundles and never log secrets.

- Negative-path coverage:
  - Ensure MFA/password-required flows reliably return NeedsHuman with clear next steps.

- Redaction tests:
  - Explicitly scan logs/artifacts for tokenized URLs and device codes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.
